senses.definition: a huge round object that moves in an orbit, around the sun, or any other star

examples.x: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system.
Earth is the only planet known to support life.
Astronomers discovered a new planet orbiting a distant star.
Saturn's rings make it one of the most visually striking planets in our solar system.

senses.definition: the different types of gases that surround the earth or other planets
the place or area in which certain animals, birds, or plants naturally exist, lives, and grows

examples.x: The habitat of a species plays a crucial role in determining its survival and reproduction rates, as well as its overall health and well-being.
Human activities such as deforestation and urbanization can lead to habitat destruction, threatening the existence of many plant and animal species.
Conservation efforts often focus on preserving and restoring habitats to maintain biodiversity and protect endangered species.

senses.definition: (usually plural) a country's gas, oil, trees, etc. that are considered valuable and therefore can be sold to gain wealth

examples.x: Renewable energy resources such as wind and solar power are gaining importance.
Environmentalists advocate for responsible use of resources to mitigate climate change.
Exploitation of marine resources has led to overfishing in some regions.

senses.definition: the energy that is obtained through different means, such as electrical or solar, to operate different equipment or machines

examples.x: Engineers are developing new ways to generate power from renewable sources.
Using wind turbines, the farm generates enough power to sustain itself.
The computer shut down suddenly due to a power surge.

senses.definition: any substance that can produce energy or heat when burned

examples.x: Wood is a common fuel for campfires.
The car ran out of fuel on the highway.
Gasoline is a widely used fuel for vehicles.
Coal has been a traditional fuel for power plants.
The fireplace was stocked with plenty of fuel to keep us warm.

senses.definition: a fuel that is found in nature and obtained from the remains of plants and animals that died millions of years ago, such as coal and gas

examples.x: Burning fossil fuels releases carbon dioxide into the atmosphere.
Coal is one of the most widely used fossil fuels.
Fossil fuels are a major source of global energy production.
Natural gas is considered a cleaner fossil fuel compared to coal.
Many cars still rely on fossil fuels like gasoline.

senses.definition: a type of fossil fuel, which is black and found in the ground, typically used as a source of energy

examples.x: Coal has been a crucial source of energy for centuries, playing a significant role in powering industries and generating electricity worldwide.
The mining and burning of coal can have detrimental environmental impacts, including air and water pollution, as well as greenhouse gas emissions contributing to climate change.
Despite efforts to transition to cleaner energy sources, coal remains an important fuel in many countries due to its abundance and affordability.

senses.definition: a liquid found deep under the ground that is used as a fuel
a source of power that can be used to produce heat, light, or to operate different machines
a clean and powerful energy that is obtained by splitting atoms, which then can be used to produce heat, electricity, etc.
the amount of carbon dioxide that an organization or person releases into the atmosphere

examples.x: Flying frequently increases your carbon footprint significantly.
Driving electric cars can help lower your carbon footprint.
Manufacturing industries often have a high carbon footprint.
The government encouraged citizens to cut their carbon footprints.
The company is working to reduce its carbon footprint by switching to renewable energy.

senses.definition: a type of gas with no color and smell that is produced by burning carbon or during breathing out

examples.x: Breathing out releases carbon dioxide into the air.
Carbon dioxide is one of the main contributors to global warming.
Plants absorb carbon dioxide during photosynthesis.
Scientists monitor carbon dioxide emissions to study climate change.
Burning fossil fuels generates carbon dioxide.

senses.definition: having no harmful substances that could cause pollution

examples.x: Clean energy sources like solar and wind power offer environmentally friendly alternatives to traditional fossil fuels, reducing pollution and greenhouse gas emissions.
Maintaining clean air quality standards is essential for protecting public health and minimizing the harmful effects of air pollution on respiratory and cardiovascular systems.
Environmental regulations and waste management practices aim to ensure clean water supplies by preventing contamination from pollutants and toxins.
Adopting sustainable practices such as recycling and reducing single-use plastics can help keep our oceans and beaches clean, preserving marine ecosystems and wildlife.

senses.definition: the act of removing harmful or dirty substances from somewhere
referring to products, actions, or practices that are designed to cause minimal harm to the environment

examples.x: The company switched to eco-friendly packaging made from recycled materials.
She prefers eco-friendly cleaning products that don’t contain harmful chemicals.
The eco-friendly car runs on electricity, reducing carbon emissions.
His home is built with eco-friendly materials to promote energy efficiency.
They installed eco-friendly solar panels to lower their energy consumption.

senses.definition: (of a substance or product) causing no harm to the environment

examples.x: The company prides itself on producing green packaging that reduces waste.
They switched to green cleaning products to ensure a safer home environment.
The green technology used in the new cars helps reduce overall emissions.
Her garden features green fertilizers that are safe for the ecosystem.
The green building design includes features such as energy-efficient windows and water-saving fixtures.

senses.definition: to damage the environment by releasing harmful chemicals or substances to the air, water, or land

examples.x: Factories often pollute the air with emissions from burning fossil fuels.
Dumping untreated sewage into rivers can pollute the water and harm aquatic life.
Pesticides used in agriculture can pollute soil and groundwater if not applied responsibly.
Cars emit exhaust fumes that pollute the atmosphere with greenhouse gases.
Oil spills from tankers polluted oceans until preventative measures were put in place.

senses.definition: to use a supply of energy, fuel, etc.

examples.x: During the winter months, households tend to consume more energy for heating to stay warm.
Electric vehicles are designed to be more efficient, consuming less energy compared to traditional gasoline-powered cars.
As the population grows, cities continue to consume vast amounts of water for residential, industrial, and agricultural needs.
Efficient appliances and lighting systems can significantly lower the amount of electricity consumed in homes.

senses.definition: an urgent situation in which proper action must be taken to remove the threats done to the environment
any destruction caused by the nature that results in a great amount of damage or the death of many, such as an earthquake, flood, etc.

examples.x: The city was devastated by a natural disaster, leaving many homeless.
A natural disaster like an earthquake can change lives in seconds.
The government responded quickly to the natural disaster with relief efforts.
The region is prone to natural disasters, including floods and tornadoes.
The tsunami was one of the deadliest natural disasters in recorded history.

senses.definition: the sudden release of lava, gases, and ash from a volcano

examples.x: The volcanic eruption sent ash clouds high into the sky.
A massive volcanic eruption devastated the nearby villages.
The volcanic eruption was felt as far as 100 miles away.
The lava from the volcanic eruption flowed into the ocean.
A volcanic eruption can significantly alter the landscape.

senses.definition: things such as household materials that have no use anymore

examples.x: He threw the garbage into the bin after dinner.
The street was full of garbage after the festival.
I need to take the garbage out before it starts to smell.
He complained about the pile of garbage in the alley.
The children were told not to leave their garbage on the beach.

senses.definition: materials that have no use and are unwanted

examples.x: Recycling helps to reduce the amount of waste sent to landfills by reusing materials such as paper, glass, and plastic.
Hazardous waste, such as chemicals and electronic waste, requires special handling and disposal methods to prevent harm to human health and the environment.
Plastic waste poses a significant threat to marine ecosystems, with millions of tons of plastic entering oceans each year and endangering marine life.

senses.definition: any type of gas, particularly carbon dioxide, that contributes to global warming by trapping heat

examples.x: Carbon dioxide is a major greenhouse gas responsible for global warming.
Methane is a potent greenhouse gas released from livestock.
The use of fossil fuels contributes to greenhouse gas pollution.
Scientists monitor greenhouse gas levels using satellite data.
Policies aim to reduce the production of greenhouse gases globally.

senses.definition: a global problem that is caused by the increase of harmful gases such as carbon dioxide which results in gradual warming of the earth

examples.x: The greenhouse effect is a natural phenomenon vital for sustaining life on Earth, but the enhanced greenhouse effect caused by human activities has accelerated climate change and its associated impacts.

senses.definition: consisting of poisonous substances

examples.x: The toxic fumes emitted by the factory posed a serious threat to the health of nearby residents.
Exposure to toxic chemicals in the workplace can have long-term health consequences for employees.
Some species of plants and animals produce toxic substances as a defense mechanism against predators.
Proper disposal of electronic waste is crucial to prevent toxic materials from leaching into the environment and contaminating soil and water sources.

senses.definition: consisting of toxic substances that can cause harm or death

examples.x: The sting of a scorpion can be poisonous, so seek medical attention if you're ever unfortunate enough to get one.
We learned to identify poisonous berries during our camping trip to avoid any accidents.
The fumes from burnt car paint can be poisonous, so always ensure proper ventilation when working on vehicles.
Certain houseplants, like lilies, are poisonous to cats, so keep them out of reach if you have feline companions.

senses.definition: toxic and harmful substances in the air that can cause illnesses

examples.x: The heavy air pollution made it difficult to breathe and caused itchy eyes for many residents.
Traffic congestion was a major contributor to the rising air pollution levels in the city.
Wearing a face mask offered some protection from inhaling harmful particles caused by air pollution.
Scientists are developing cleaner energy sources to combat air pollution and its negative impact on the environment.
Public awareness campaigns encouraged people to use public transportation or carpool to reduce their contribution to air pollution.

senses.definition: a cloud of chemicals produced by burning something

examples.x: Smoke rose from the chimney as the fire burned inside.
The smoke from the campfire made her eyes water.
Thick smoke filled the room during the fire.
He saw black smoke coming from the factory.
The chef waved the smoke away from the pan.

senses.definition: a large building in which electricity is made

examples.x: The massive hydroelectric power plant harnessed the power of the rushing river to generate clean electricity.
The old coal-fired power plant was being decommissioned due to concerns about air pollution.
Scientists are researching ways to make geothermal power plants more efficient to tap into the Earth's natural heat for energy production.

senses.definition: to make a waste product usable again

examples.x: Recycling paper involves collecting and processing used paper products to make new paper.
Many communities encourage residents to recycle plastic bottles to reduce environmental pollution.
Composting is a method of recycling organic waste to create nutrient-rich soil for gardening.
Electronic waste can be recycled to recover valuable materials and reduce electronic waste pollution.

senses.definition: the process of making waste products usable again

examples.x: Recycling helps reduce environmental pollution.
He teaches children about the benefits of recycling.
Factories use advanced recycling methods for metals.
The school organized a recycling campaign.
The city introduced a new recycling program.

senses.definition: (of a resource, energy, etc.) naturally restored as fast as or faster than they are used up

examples.x: Geothermal energy, derived from the heat of the Earth's core, is a renewable source of heat and electricity.

senses.definition: an unexpected and usually dangerous situation needing immediate attention or action

examples.x: The lifeguard quickly responded to the emergency when a swimmer began struggling in the water.
The pilot declared an emergency due to engine failure.
The school conducted a drill to prepare students for a fire emergency.
He called 911 when he realized the flooding in his basement was an emergency.
The sudden power outage was treated as an emergency by the utility company.

senses.definition: to become destroyed, often due to the action of bacteria or fungi over time

examples.x: If not refrigerated, food can quickly rot.
The damp wood is currently rotting in the neglected shed.
The fallen tree was rotting away, returning nutrients to the soil.
The neglected vegetables in the compost bin are currently rotting, turning into nutrient-rich soil.

senses.definition: any substance that is dirty, disgusting, or unpleasant

examples.x: The abandoned building was overrun with filth, its floors littered with garbage and its walls coated in layers of grime.
Despite efforts to maintain cleanliness, the alleyway was a breeding ground for filth, attracting pests and emitting foul odors.
The restaurant's kitchen was shut down by health inspectors due to its egregious violations of sanitation standards, citing widespread filth and contamination.
The detective carefully sifted through the filth of the crime scene, searching for clues amidst the chaos and disorder.

senses.definition: to cause a particular thing to decay or decompose

examples.x: The damp conditions in the basement rotted the wooden beams, causing structural damage to the house.
Neglecting to refrigerate the leftovers promptly can rot the food.
Excessive watering of plants can rot their roots, depriving them of oxygen and causing them to decay in the soil.
The lack of airflow in the storage room rotted the fabric of the old books.
Inadequate drainage around the foundation can rot the building materials, leading to water damage and deterioration of the structure.
